name: Sinnit
description: A brutish Purewater canal-rat and rival thief who nurses a hard grudge against Randall -- a brawling enforcer-type with a long memory and a short temper.
narrative: Sinnit works the same Purewater underworld as Randall, but where Randall is quick and charming, Sinnit is heavy-handed and mean. A canal-rat brawler and small-time crew muscle, he blames Randall for an old double-cross -- a job gone wrong, a cut he never got, a humiliation he never forgot -- and he has been looking to settle the score ever since. He hits hard, fights dirty, and holds a grudge like a creditor.

equipment.name: Leather jerkin (all locations)
equipment.notes: AP 1 each location; no armor penalty

extras.class_note: Street-level brawler-thief: muscle over finesse (Unarmed 70, Intimidation 68, Brawn 58) with rogue trimmings (Stealth 55, Streetwise 66). No magic.
extras.role: Purewater canal-rat / rival crew muscle; a recurring local enemy of Randall.
extras.relationship: Local enemy of Randall -- holds a grudge over an old double-cross / unpaid cut; spoiling for payback.
abilities: Hide in Shadows (Perception to spot is 1 grade harder)
extras.authored_note: New NPC -- no Roll20 sheet. GM-authored from the brief ('a local enemy of Randall: Sinnit'). The grudge hook (old double-cross) is a suggestion -- adjust the cause to taste. Tune freely.
extras.source: Authored for the And then the Dragons Came campaign (enemy of Randall)
